fields.label: Relay Target ID
fields.description: The shard key; the container/gateway this pump serves. The admission serializer takes SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on it (OAR-1).

fields.label: Transport Mode
fields.description: CDX-10 — the DB-AUTHORITATIVE transport decision for this shard: which machine owns a NEW turn's dispatch. 'pump' = the Relay Pump owns dispatch; 'draining' = no new pump admissions (new turns fall through to legacy) while existing pump Turn rows drain; 'legacy' = the kill switch (byte-identical worker-per-turn). This ROW — read under the shard control-row FOR UPDATE — is the fenced decision value at every dispatch-deciding point (accept_or_queue's pump branch, _dispatch_turn's enqueue-boundary re-check); jarvis_pump_enabled in site_config is the operator-facing MIRROR only, never the decider (an already-initialized request's frappe.local.conf can be stale across a cutover). Empty = not yet reconciled; readers fall back to the config-derived mode until pump_cutover_execute / a reconcile writes it.

fields.label: Mode Epoch
fields.description: CDX-10 — the transport_mode fencing generation. Advanced (+1) under the shard control-row FOR UPDATE on every DELIBERATE transport_mode change (pump_cutover_execute and any operator-driven mode flip), so a mode transition is observable/orderable and a stale actor's cached mode can be fenced. Distinct from pump_epoch (which fences pump LEASE ownership).

fields.label: Mirror Mismatch
fields.description: CDX-21 — the operator-facing signal that the site_config jarvis_pump_enabled MIRROR currently disagrees with this row's authoritative transport_mode (a mirror write failed after the row committed, or a crash cut between the commit and the mirror write). The ROW still decides all admission + lifecycle behaviour regardless; the watchdog rewrites the config from the row each cycle and clears this flag once they agree. Purely informational — never read for a decision.

fields.label: Pump Epoch
fields.description: The MONOTONIC fencing generation. Advanced transactionally on EVERY lease acquisition (cold start, takeover, self-hop). A pump caches E; every pump-owned write CASes WHERE pump_epoch=E; a stale pump's E no longer matches so it affects 0 rows and exits (D2 §3, D4).

fields.label: Lease Holder
fields.description: Current hop's RQ job id (jarvis-pump::<site>::hop<n>); diagnostic + takeover logging (D2 §3).

fields.label: Lease Expires At
fields.description: Lease TTL wall-clock (~30s, heartbeat ~10s). A takeover is legal only when now > lease_expires_at OR the lease is vacant. MariaDB is authoritative; Redis mirrors for the fast wake path (D2 §3). Kept NULLABLE (D2 §3 states NOT NULL) so WP-0's bare _ensure_control_row insert stays valid; NULL = never-leased/vacant, treated as acquirable by lease_acquire's predicate.

fields.label: Loop Heartbeat Ts
fields.description: Loop-WRITTEN liveness, DISTINCT from lease renewal (Amendment E). Catches the live-but-wedged pump the lease alone cannot (D2 §3).

fields.label: Hop Counter
fields.description: The <n> in the hop job id; increments on every self-requeue so the new hop gets a FRESH job id (Frappe job-id dedupe trap). Separate from pump_epoch: hop_counter mints ids, pump_epoch fences writes (D2 §3).
